loanSummary.active: {count, plural, one {1 active loan} other {# active loans}}

loadError.message: Could not load your finances. Please try again.

take.title: Take out a loan
take.noConfig: No loan configuration available for Division {division}
take.maxBorrowableDetail: The fee of {fee} CZ$ is added to your debt, so it reaches {total} CZ$ of the {ceiling} CZ$ cap.
take.maxZero: No room for a new loan. Your debt is at the division cap of {ceiling} CZ$.
take.exceedsMax: This would push you over the debt cap, because the fee counts towards it. Max loan right now: {value} CZ$.

balance.title: Your balance and cashflow
balance.body: Balance changes from sponsor (income at season start), salaries (paid at the start of the next season after sponsor lands), transfers, and prize money (ongoing). Red numbers mean negative balance, and the emergency-loan mechanism kicks in at the next season start if sponsor and balance cannot cover salary and interest.

debtCeiling.title: Debt cap per division
debtCeiling.body: Each division has a cap on total debt (D1 1,200K · D2 900K · D3 600K). Once the cap is reached, new loans are blocked, and the board also penalises overspending at season evaluation.

txHistory.title: Follow the cashflow
txHistory.body: Sponsor and salary are one-off per season (both at season start: sponsor first, then salary), while prize money and transfers come in continuously. Here you see every transaction, use it to track whether the finances are heading the right way.

hint.title: How your finances work
hint.subtitle: Four income and expense streams drive your balance. Understand them, and you can prioritise sport, growth, and debt.

sponsor.title: Sponsor income
sponsor.before: Scaled by division (D1 600,000 · D2 400,000 · D3 260,000 CZ$) per season × your
sponsor.after: . Paid as a lump sum at season start.

salary.title: Rider salaries
salary.body: 6.7% of the rider's market value per season (base value plus prize money bonus). Deducted as a lump sum at the start of the next season, right after the sponsor lands. Star riders are expensive to keep.

debt.title: Debt cap per division
debt.body: D1 1,200K · D2 900K · D3 600K. Break the cap and new loans are blocked, and the board punishes overspending.

loans.title: Loans, short vs long
loans.body: Short loan: low fee, fast repayment. Long loan: larger amount across several seasons. Use them for transfer peaks, not operating gaps.

report.title: Finance report · season {n}
report.sponsorBody: Available from season 2. Requires board plan history from at least one closed season.
